title: Pentagon Secures Classified AI Deals with OpenAI, Google, Nvidia — Excludes Anthropic
summary: The Pentagon has signed agreements with leading AI firms to use their tools in classified settings, notably excluding Anthropic. This move underscores the growing importance of AI in defense and national security.
body:
The Pentagon has inked classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, Elon Musk's xAI, and startup Reflection. This announcement marks a significant step in integrating advanced AI capabilities into national defense operations. Notably absent from the list is Anthropic, which had previously been involved in classified projects with the Defense Department.

The inclusion of major tech players like OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia highlights the Pentagon's strategic focus on leveraging cutting-edge AI for defense applications. These agreements allow the agency to access and utilize AI tools in secure, classified environments, ensuring that sensitive military operations can benefit from the latest advancements. The exclusion of Anthropic raises questions about the company's role and future in defense contracts.

Moving forward, the Pentagon's partnerships with these AI giants are expected to accelerate the development and deployment of AI-driven solutions for defense. The absence of Anthropic from these deals could signal a shift in the Pentagon's preferences or a strategic realignment in the AI landscape. The industry will be watching closely to see how these agreements impact national security and the broader AI market.
